artifact: Career-finder COMP row: 'Posting quoted: "Pay: $24.00/hour, full-time 40 hrs/week." Annualized: $24/hr x 2,080 = $58,240/yr. Floor is $55,000. $58,240 > $55,000, so the base clears the floor. Verdict: PASS.' The job-seeker's stated minimum base is $55,000/year and the role is 40 hrs/week.
failure_mode: fallacy-oversight bias (judge accepts a clean-looking final conclusion without auditing the arithmetic step)
source: arxiv.org/html/2410.02736v1 -- "LLM judges may ignore logical errors in reasoning steps and only focus on the correctness of final results." ; fix_plan.md H6 -- "BASE row must ANNUALIZE an hourly range (rate x 2080) and test the BOTTOM of the band against the floor."
why_hard: The report shows its work and the final inequality reads as a clean PASS, so a judge that trusts the displayed conclusion will not re-multiply 24 x 2080.
objective_fact: $24.00/hr x 2,080 = $49,920, not $58,240; the true annualized base is below the $55,000 floor, so the correct verdict is FAIL. (2,080 is correct for 40 hrs/wk; the multiplication in the artifact is wrong.)
